name: Old-Fashioned Chocolate Cake with Cocoa Frost
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Unsalted Butter, plus additional for spreading
3/4 cup Plus 3 tablespoons Unsweetened Cocoa Powder
recipeInstructions:
For Cake: Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Butter and flour three 9-inch-diameter cake pans with 1-1/2 inch-high sides. In a medium bowl, sift the first five ingredients together. In a large bowl, mix the water, oil, and vanilla extract. Whisk in the dry ingredients until just combined. Divide the batter among the prepared pans. Sprinkle half a cup of chocolate chips over the batter in each pan. Bake cakes until a tester inserted into the center comes out clean, about 25 minutes. Cool cakes in the pans on wire racks for 15 minutes. Cut around the pan sides to loosen the cakes. Turn the cakes out onto serving plates; cool completely.
For Frosting: In a large bowl, beat the butter until creamy. Gradually whisk in the powdered sugar and vanilla extract. Beat in the milk. Add the cocoa powder and remaining powdered sugar; beat until blended, adding more milk if necessary to achieve desired consistency. Place one cake layer with the chocolate-chip side up on a serving platter. Spread about two-thirds of the frosting over the cake. Top with the second cake layer, chocolate-chip side up, and spread another two-thirds of the frosting over it. Finish by topping with the remaining cake layer, chocolate-chip side down, and spreading the frosting over the sides and top of the cake.
